articles.title: Article 1
articles.text:
Section 1 of the Regulations is amended by: 1. replacing the definition of "competent authority" with the following: "competent authority": within the meaning of the Code and these Regulations, the Director of the Planning and Mobility Service; in the Code, a reference to the "Building Authority" or "the Board" must be understood as a reference to the competent authority when it is a requirement for which the city has jurisdiction; and 2. replacing the definition of "exempt building" with the following: "exempt building":
Building of the Quebec Construction Code by section 1.04 of the Quebec Construction Code;"; 3. the replacement of the definition of "Code" by the following: "Code": the National Building Code – Canada 2010 (NRC 53301E) published by the Canadian Commission on Building and Fire Codes of the National Research Council of Canada, as adopted and amended by Order-in-Council 953-2000, 293-2008 and 347-2015, also known as the Quebec Construction Code, (RLRQ, c. B-1.1, r.2) (Chapter I, Building, and National Building Code – Canada 2010 (amended)).
This Code is attached to Schedule A to these Regulations;"; 4° l , added after the definition of "construction", the following paragraph: "Unless otherwise specified, the terms "construction" or "built" used for the purpose of the construction action include the terms "transformation" or "converted"; 5° l, added after the definition of "construction", the following definition: "habitation space" means a space or room intended for the preparation or consumption of meals, sleep or living room, excluding, but not limited to, a bathroom, toilet, storage, wardrobe and laundry room;";
6° l-Add, after the definition of "flooding plain", the following definitions: "useful surface" means the surface of living spaces measured between the faces of walls that are in accordance with the minimum heights of Table 9.5.3.1 of Division B of the Code excluding the surface occupied by kitchen cabinets, the floor area intended for use by a stove, refrigerator, freezer, washing machine and dryer, the dressers incorporated and the hangings; "vegetated roof" means a part of a roof covering system that is designed to allow vegetation to grow; "Type 1 vegetated roof" means:
"Vegetable roof" means a vegetated roof with a growth substrate of more than 150 mm in thickness;"

articles.title: Article 2
articles.text: Section 3 of these Regulations is replaced by the following: "3. Unless otherwise specified, these Regulations apply to all buildings and buildings, both those to which the Code applies under the Building Act (QLR, c. B-1.1) and those exempted from the application of Chapter I of Division I-Building of the Quebec Construction Code by section 1.04 of the Code."

articles.title: Article 3
articles.text: Section 4 of these Regulations is replaced by the following: "4. Subject to section 4.2 and with the exception of Part 11 of Division B, the Code applies to an exempt vessel. Any amendment or new edition of the Code applies in respect of an exempt vessel from the date established by resolution of the City Council."

articles.title: Article 4
articles.text: These Regulations are amended by adding after section 4 the following sections: "4.1. Subject to section 13, the construction or alteration of a building shall be carried out in accordance with the provisions of these Regulations and the relevant acceptable solutions of Division B of the Code.

articles.title: Article 4.2
articles.text: The requirements of the 2017 NFPA-130 Standard – "Standard for Fixed Guideway Transit and Passenger Rail Systems" attached to Appendix B to these Regulations replace the Code requirements for all construction and transformation work at metro stations."

articles.title: Article 5
articles.text: Section 5 of these Regulations is replaced by the following: "5. The sections of this Division apply to all buildings."

articles.title: Article 6
articles.text: Section 7 of these Regulations is replaced by the following: "7. A wall constructed at the property boundary not bordering a highway, except that of a garage or a secondary building referred to in paragraphs 4 and 5 of section 9.10.14.5. of Division B of the Code, shall be covered by a concrete or masonry wall bound by mortar, not less than 90 mm thick, and shall have at least half the fire resistance required for a fire-resistant wall required according to the intended primary use."

articles.title: Article 7
articles.text: Section 8 of these Regulations is amended to replace the words "d-a snow-shield or any other necessary device or system" with the words "d-a device".

articles.title: Article 8
articles.text: The Regulations are amended by adding, after section 9, the following section: "9.1. Clause 4 of section 9.10.914. of Division B of the Code relating to the possibility of replacing fire-stop separations with smoke-tight barriers, for buildings of up to 3 dwellings and up to 2 floors, do not apply."

articles.title: Article 9
articles.text: Article 10 of the Regulations is amended by: 1° the addition of the words "and floor area of garages" in the first paragraph after the words "part of garages"; 2° the replacement of the words "free space" in the third paragraph of the second paragraph by the words "free space" in the third paragraph of the second paragraph; 3° the replacement of the word "free air" in the third paragraph of the third paragraph of the first paragraph of the first paragraph of the first paragraph of the first paragraph of the first paragraph of the first paragraph of the first paragraph of the first paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph of the second paragraph

articles.title: Article 10
articles.text: Article 11 of the Regulation is amended by inserting the words "and paragraph 1 of Article 3.2.5.6" after the words "Article 3.2.5.5."

articles.title: Article 11
articles.text: These Regulations are amended by adding, after section 11, the following section: "11.1. Except in the case of a horizontal exit in accordance with sections 3.4.1.6 and 3.4.6.10. of Division B of the Code, any route of a required exit from a vessel shall be situated entirely on the property it serves."

articles.title: Article 12
articles.text: Section 13 of the Regulations is replaced by the following: "13. If the competent authority is shown that the conditions of accommodation and occupancy of a building and the requirements set out in the Code cannot be reasonably applied, the Code may apply different measures or alternatives to those conditions if it is of the opinion that such measures or solutions provide a sufficient degree of safety and security.

articles.title: Article 13
articles.text: Article 14 of that Regulation is amended by deleting the third paragraph.

articles.title: Article 14
articles.text: These Regulations are amended by adding after section 14 the following sections: "14.1. Despite section 14, natural second-day lighting of a room in a dwelling is permitted when the following conditions are met in addition to the requirements set out in subparagraphs (a) and (b) of paragraph 4 of section 9.7.2.3. of Division B of the Code: 1° second-day illuminated area and 1° second-day illuminated area with the glass surface providing natural lighting form parts combined within the meaning of the Code; 2° opening between the two areas shall be free from obstacles; 3° the glass surface providing natural lighting shall be at least 10% of the total surface of the combined rooms.

articles.title: Article 14.2
articles.text: No projection that can intercept light, such as a balcony, cornice or other construction, located less than 1.5 m above a required window shall project over more than 2 m, unless the minimum required glazed area is increased according to Table 14.2. TABLE 14.2 Percentage of fenestration with projection capable of intercepting light Projection of projection 2 m and less 2 m - 2.5m - 2.5m -3m 3m -3.5m 3.5 -4m more than 4m % fenestration required according to Article 14 8 9 10 11 12 13 5 5 6 7 8 9 10 10 10 11 12 13 14 15

articles.title: Article 14.3
articles.text: Any glass surface required shall give a free clearance in accordance with the following conditions: 1o be at least 1.5 m deep over a width equal to the width of glazed surface but not less than 1.5 m; 2o be open from the top of the glazed surface; 3o be adjacent to or separated from the glazed surface by a clear space, balcony, gallery or open staircase; this clearance shall not encroach outside the lateral or rear limits of the ground, but may encroach on the open road; Cornices and foregates may encroach on the clearance at a maximum depth of 600 mm.

articles.title: Article 14.4
articles.text: For each room of a dwelling with all rooms located below the floor level or a bedroom house with a portion of the free height below the floor level, the floor level in front of each required window shall be at least 1 m below the ceiling of that room. The floor level shall be determined without regard to localized depressions such as loupes.

articles.title: Article 14.5
articles.text: When a new dwelling is fitted, the support of the windows required by section 9.9.10.1 of Division B of the Code, which are intended to be used as an emergency exit to a room, shall be not more than 1.5 m from the floor."

articles.title: Article 15
articles.text: Section 16 of these Regulations is amended by replacing the words "in Table 9.6.3.1." with the words "in Table 9.5.5.1."

articles.title: Article 16
articles.text: Section 17 of these Regulations is amended by adding the following two paragraphs after the last paragraph: "Bathrooms and toilets in a room house shall be in separate rooms, which shall be accessible without the need to climb or descend more than one floor from the rooms served. When a room in a room house is provided with one or more of the following sanitary appliances: W.C., bath or shower, they shall be installed in a closed space."

articles.title: Article 17
articles.text: These Regulations are amended by adding after section 17 the following sections: "17.1. The requirements of Part 9 of Division B of the Code apply to all dwellings in respect of: 1o sanitary equipment; 2o lighting; 3o heating.

articles.title: Article 17.2
articles.text: Inside a dwelling, at least one toilet, bathtub or shower, and a washbasin shall be located in one or more enclosed spaces.

articles.title: Article 17.3
articles.text: A room or space for the preparation of meals shall be fitted with: 1o a low cabinet with a working surface of at least 1 m2, excluding the surface occupied by the sink; 2o a cabinet with a volume of at least 1.25 m3, including that required by paragraph 1."

articles.title: Article 18
articles.text:
Article 18 of that Regulation is replaced by the following: "18. The surfaces and dimensions of areas and rooms of a dwelling shall have a useful surface of at least 1o 13.5 m2, with no dimension less than 3 m, if it is a living area, whether it is a separate room or a combined area; 2o 3.25 m2, with no dimension less than 2.3 m measured between the face of the walls or between the face of a wall and the face of an incorporated wardrobe or apparatus, if it is a dining area combined with another area;
3o 7 m2, with no dimension less than 2.3 m measured between the faces of the walls or between the face of a wall and that of an incorporated cabinet or apparatus, if it is a single dining room; 4o 3.2 m2, with no dimension less than 2.3 m, if it is a kitchen, whether isolated or combined with other areas, except in dwellings where only one or two persons can sleep, in which case the minimum useful surface is 2.7 m2, with no dimension less than 1.7 m; 5o 9.8 m2, with no dimension less than 2.7 m, if it is the master bedroom; 6o 7 m2, with no dimension less than 2 m, if it is a chamber other than the master bedroom."

articles.title: Article 19
articles.text: These Regulations are amended by adding after section 18 the following sections: "18.1. The corridor inside a dwelling shall be at least 900 mm wide.

articles.title: Article 18.2
articles.text: A separate 2 living rooms unit must have a useful area of at least 20m2 and a minimum additional area of 3m2 for each additional living room.

articles.title: Article 18.3
articles.text: The useful surface of a bedroom of a room house must be at least 9 m2, unless the room is occupied by more than one person, in which case the useful surface must be at least 7 m2 per person and the room house must be provided with a common space such as a kitchen or living room."

articles.title: Article 20
articles.text:
The Regulations are amended by adding after section 19 the following section: "19.1. A temporary storage space for residual materials shall be fitted in any building: 1° built after (indicate the date of entry into force of these Regulations) and occupied by more than 11 dwellings; 2° modified for residential use after (indicate the date of entry into force of these Regulations) and comprising more than 11 dwellings; 3° processed resulting in an increase in building height, building floor or floor floor area and comprising more than 11 dwellings after construction;
4° having an establishment having an area of 100 m2 or more where food is prepared or consumed and which is either (i) fitted out after (indicate the date of entry into force of these Regulations); (ii) redeveloped after (indicate the date of entry into force of these Regulations) and whose value of the work exceeds $100,000."

articles.title: Article 21
articles.text: Article 20 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following subparagraph after subparagraph 4(d): "(e) the pumping capacity to discharge the water from infiltration."

articles.title: Article 22
articles.text: Article 21 of the Regulations is amended by: 1° add, after paragraph 2o of the following paragraph: "3° A rainfall shall not be connected directly or indirectly to the drainage pipe of the foundation soles."; 2° add, after the first subparagraph, the following paragraph: "Despite paragraph 1° of the first subparagraph, the drainage pipe may be installed inside the foundations in the case of a wall or an external wall built at the property boundary."

articles.title: Article 23
articles.text: These Regulations are amended by adding, after section 25, the following subsection and sections: "SECTION VII SUSTAINABLE CONSTRUCTION" 25.1. The roof structure of an uncombustible building of more than 3 floors in building height and more than 250 m2 shall, when the roof slope is less than 2 units vertically in 12 units on the horizontal (2:12) or 16.7%, be designed to receive a type 1 green roof in addition to the loads prescribed by the Code.

articles.title: Article 25.2
articles.text: When an increase in the area of buildings of more than 250 m2 to an uncombustible building of more than 3 floors in building height, the roof structure of the enlarged part must, when the slope of roof is less than 2 units vertically in 12 units horizontal (2:12) or 16.7%, be designed to receive a type 1 vegetated roof in addition to the loads prescribed by the Code.

articles.title: Article 25.3
articles.text: Articles 25.1 and 25.2 do not apply to roofs: 1° of a roof-out construction; 2° of a mezzanine whose area does not exceed 40% of the floor that it overcomes.

articles.title: Article 25.4
articles.text: The total thermal strength of the roof of a building having a primary use of Group C, as defined in the Code, shall be equal to or greater than RSIT 9.0 (R-51).

articles.title: Article 25.5
articles.text: Despite the provisions of Part 11 of the Code, window-doors, windows and window-doors serving a dwelling must comply with the "Energystar" certification according to the criteria established by Natural Resources Canada for Climate Zone 2 attached to Appendix C to these Regulations."

articles.title: Article 24
articles.text: Section 26 of these Regulations is replaced by the following: "26. Despite any other requirement of Parts 1 to 10 of Division B of the Code, the sections of this Division apply to exempt vessels."

articles.title: Article 25
articles.text: These Regulations are amended by adding the following subsections and sections after section 27: "Sub-SECTION I SUSTAINABLE CONSTRUCTION – TOO VEGETALIZED

articles.title: Article 27.1
articles.text: The roof structure of a building with a height of more than 3 floors and a height of more than 250 m2 shall, when the roof slope is less than 2 units vertically in 12 units horizontally (2:12) or 16.7%, be designed to receive a type 1 green roof in addition to the loads prescribed by the Code.

articles.title: Article 27.2
articles.text: When an increase in the area of a building of more than 250 m2 to a building with a building height of more than 3 floors, the structure of the roof of the enlarged part shall, when the slope of the roof is less than 2 units vertically in 12 units horizontally (2:12) or 16.7%, be designed to receive a type 1 green roof in addition to the loads prescribed by the Code.

articles.title: Article 27.3
articles.text: Articles 27.1 and 27.2 do not apply to roofs: 1° of a roof-out construction; 2° of a mezzanine whose area does not exceed 40% of the floor that it overcomes.

articles.title: Article 27.4
Article 27.5
articles.text: Despite section 3.1.10.7, paragraph 2, of Division B of the Code, a fuel project of a fuel vessel may be within 1.2 m of the axis of the firewall or a property boundary in the following cases: 1o the projection is 1m or less above ground level; 2o the building has a maximum of 2 housing units and the projection is not less than 1.2 m from the axis of another firewall or other property boundary not bordering a highway.

articles.title: Article 27.6
articles.text: Despite articles 27.5 and 3.2.3.6, paragraph 6 of article 9.10.14.5. and paragraph 5 of article 9.10.15.5. of division B of the Code, handrails and stairs on an external wall facing a street serving a floor area not more than 1 floor above the first floor may be of a fuel-built type.

articles.title: Article 27.7
articles.text: The guardrails required by section 9.8.8.1. of Division B of the Code, serving a non-industrial use and protecting a level at a height of not more than 4.2 m above the adjacent level may not meet the requirements for the design of guardrails in section 3.3.1.18, paragraph 3, and section 9.8.8.6. of Division B of the Code.

articles.title: Article 27.8
Article 27.9
articles.text: Despite the second paragraph of Article 14.1, in a fire-proof suite with a complete mechanical ventilation system including the second-day illuminated area, the opening between the two areas forming combined parts may be fitted with doors provided that they do not reduce the area of the prescribed opening when they are in an open position."

articles.title: Article 26
Article 27
articles.text: These Regulations are amended by adding the following heading after section 28: "Sub-SECTION IV AVERAGE AVERAGE".

articles.title: Article 28
articles.text: These Regulations are amended by adding after section 29 the following sections: "29.1. Despite section 9.8.3.1 of Division B of the Code, an outside staircase that serves only dwellings and that does not constitute their only exit may be turned in full or rotated in one or more parts on the following conditions: 1° it serves a level not more than one floor above the first floor; 2° it serves not more than 2 units per floor; 3° it has equal giras of not less than 225 mm when measured at 500 mm from the narrowest end; 4° the rotation between 2 levels is carried out in the same direction.

articles.title: Article 29.2
articles.text: Notwithstanding section 9.8.3.1. of Division B of the Code, a helical staircase authorized under subsections 3 or 4 of section 9.8.4.5. of Division B of the Code may be turned in whole or in one or more parts where each rotating part allows the rotation of at least 180 degrees.

articles.title: Article 29.3
articles.text: Despite section 9.8.2.1. of Division B of the Code, where a dwelling has at least 2 means of evacuation, one of the accommodation may be an outside staircase with a minimum free width of 760 mm, on the following conditions: 1° stairs shall be used only for housing; 2° stairs shall be used for up to 2 units per floor area.

articles.title: Article 29.4
articles.text: Despite paragraph 1 of Article 9.8.4.3 of Division B of the Code, it is not mandatory that dancing steps of an outer exit staircase serving not more than two housing units per floor conform to Article 3.4.6.9 of Division B of the Code, if they meet the following conditions: 1° they have a giron of at least 150 mm measured at the narrowest point; 2° they have a giron of at least 230 mm measured at 300 mm from the narrowest point; 3° they rotate in the same direction."

articles.title: Article 29
articles.text: The Regulations are amended by inserting the following heading after new Article 29.4: "Subsidiarity V MISCELLANEOUS PROVISIONS".

articles.title: Article 30
articles.text: Section 30 of the Regulations is amended by replacing the words "must satisfy all the following requirements" in the first paragraph with the words "may derogate from section 10.4.1.3. of Division B of the Code if the following requirements are met".

articles.title: Article 31
articles.text:
Article 31 of that Regulation is amended by replacing the first paragraph with the following:
"In respect of a transformation other than an expansion into a building area, floor area or building height, to a building or part of a building that has been issued a building permit before (indicate the date of entry into force of these Regulations), the requirements for the fit-up of rooms and safety set out in Chapter VII, IX and Chapter X.I, Division I of the City of Montreal's Safe, Maintenance and Safety of Housing Regulations (03-096) may be applied instead of those set out in the Code, in any of the following situations:".

articles.title: Article 32
articles.text: Article 32 of that Regulation is amended by deleting the words "who is" in paragraph 3 of the first subparagraph.

articles.title: Article 33
articles.text: Section 33 of the Regulations is replaced by the following: " 33. Despite section 32, a permit is not required: 1° for the installation or construction of a temporary building on a site necessary for the carrying out of construction work or used for the sale or rental of housing units; 2° for the installation, construction or expansion of a secondary building with a total area of not more than 15 m2. "

articles.title: Article 34
Article 35
articles.text: Article 35 of the Regulations is replaced by the following: "35. The Executive Committee may determine by order: (1) the form and content of the document entitled "building sheet/declaration of conformity" required under subparagraph 6(a) of the second paragraph of Article 34; (2) the form and content of the document entitled "vegetated roof sheet" required under subparagraph 5(a) of the third paragraph of Article 34.".

articles.title: Article 36
articles.text: Section 36 of these Regulations is amended by adding the following paragraph after the second paragraph: "The requirement of the first paragraph does not apply where the amendment to the description of the work results from a comment or an application for amendment from a decision-making body or from a City Advisory Committee."

articles.title: Article 37
articles.text: Section 38 of the Regulations is amended by: 1. the replacement in subsections 1°, 2o and 4o of the first paragraph of the alphanumeric designation (R.S.Q., c. A-19.1) by the following alphanumeric designation " (RLRQ, c. A-19.1); 2. the replacement in paragraph 3° of the first paragraph of the alphanumeric designation (R.S.Q., c. Q-2) by the following alphanumeric designation " (RLRQ, c. Q-2); 3. the addition after paragraph 5° of the first paragraph of the paragraph of the following paragraph: "6° the application for a permit shall be accompanied by any authorization required under a by-law."

articles.title: Article 38
articles.text: Article 40 of the Regulation is replaced by the following: "40. Upon receipt of a written request from the owner or his representative, submitted before the expiry of the expiry period, the competent authority may extend the periods of 18 and 36 months referred to in paragraph 3 of the first subparagraph of Article 39, respectively, by 6 and 12 months."

articles.title: Article 39
articles.text: Section 53 of the Regulations is replaced by the following: "53. The owner and, if applicable, the contractor shall: 1. ensure that plans and specifications as approved in respect of the work covered by the permit are available on the site of the work at any time during working hours for inspection by the competent authority; 2. post the permit or a true copy of the permit on the site in a visible manner throughout the duration of the work; 3. inform the competent authority of the commencement of the work."

articles.title: Article 40
articles.text: Section 56 of the Regulations is replaced by the following: "56. The competent authority may, by notice, order the owner, the contractor or any other interested person to suspend construction work: 1° without a permit; 2° not in accordance with the permit issued; 3° not in accordance with the regulations."

articles.title: Article 41
articles.text: Subsection II of Section III of Chapter III of these Regulations is amended by replacing the heading "CERTIFICATE OF LOCATION" with the following heading: "CERTIFICATES AND CERTIFICATES".

articles.title: Article 42
articles.text: Section 57 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following paragraph after paragraph 6°: "7° any other rating or measure to verify that the work complies with the applicable municipal regulations."

articles.title: Article 43
articles.text: Article 58 of the Regulation is replaced by the following: "58. Following construction work in a flood-risk area, the owner shall submit to the competent authority a certificate of conformity of the construction with the immunisation measures provided for in Article 20. This certificate shall be produced by a person competent in this field."

articles.title: Article 44
articles.text: The Regulations are amended by adding after section 60 the following section: "60.1. When developing a green roof, the owner shall produce to the competent authority a copy of the report of the leakproofness test required by section 27.4, paragraph 31o".

articles.title: Article 45
articles.text: Section 62 of these Regulations is amended by replacing the words "in accordance with the Code and the regulations in force at the time of such reconstruction or rehabilitation" with the words "in accordance with these Regulations".

articles.title: Article 46
articles.text: Section 69 of these Regulations is replaced by the following: "69. Every person who contravenes section 68 commits an offence and is liable to: (a) for a first offence, a fine of $1,000; (b) for a recidivism, a fine of $2,000; and (b) for a first offence, a fine of $2,000; and (c) for a recidivism, a fine of $4,000."

articles.title: Article 47
articles.text: Article 70 of that Regulation is repealed.

articles.title: Article 48
articles.text: Section 72 of these Regulations is amended by adding the following paragraph after the first paragraph: "The normative requirements of the Regulations in force before the coming into force of an amendment to these Regulations may be used in lieu of those provided for in the new Regulations, provided that the application for a licence, including all accompanying documents required by these Regulations, is submitted to the City for approval within 6 months of the coming into force of this regulatory amendment."

articles.title: Article 49
articles.text:
These Regulations are amended to add the following schedules: ANNEX A: Quebec Construction Code, (QLR, c. B-1.1, r.2) (Chapter I, Building, and National Building Code – Canada 2010 (amended)). ANNEX B: NFPA-130 – 2017 edition, "Standard for Fixed Guideway Transit and Passenger Rail Systems". ANNEX C: Energystar Certification (criteria established by Natural Resources Canada for the Climate Zone 2). ANNEX D: ANSI/GRHC/SPRI VR-1 2011 "Procedure for investigating resistance to root thought on vegetative roofs". ANNEX E:
ASTM E2397-11 "Standard practice for determination of dead loads and live loads associated with vegetable (green) roof systems" and ASTM E2399-11 "Standard test method for maximum media density for dead load analysis of vegetable (green) root systems". ANNEX F: ANSI/SPRI RP-14 "Wind Design Standard for Vegetative Roofing Systems". ANNEX G: ASTM E2398-11 "Standard Test Method for Water Capture and Media Retention of Geo-composite Drain Layers for Green Roof Systems". _____________________________ This regulation was promulgated by the Public Notice posted at City Hall and published in Le Devoir on ..... GDD 1184188001
